sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right knee pain following a fall.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports right knee pain following a fall yesterday while changing a light bulb on a ladder. He states he 'kinda stumbled' and twisted his knee. He denies any other injuries. He reports the pain is primarily on the inside of his knee and worsens with bending and twisting. He has been applying ice and taking ibuprofen, but reports minimal relief. He also reports a previous nose job.
Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation over the medial aspect of the right knee. Range of motion is limited by pain, with increased pain on forward flexion and valgus stress. Sensation is intact. X-ray of the right knee reveals no fractures but demonstrates mild joint effusion. The clinician observes tenderness and limited range of motion.
Assessment
The patient presents with a probable acute medial meniscus sprain or strain secondary to a recent knee injury.
Plan
The clinician recommends a knee brace and crutches for the next couple of days. The doctor orders 800 mg of ibuprofen to be taken every six hours as needed. A follow-up appointment is scheduled in one week to reassess the patient's condition and consider an MRI if symptoms do not improve. The clinician orders the medications and procedures discussed.
